query: Set up a pre-push hook that blocks pushing secrets.
expected_behavior: Checks betterleaks is installed (gives the install hint if not)
Installs the bundled git-hooks/pre-push into a tracked .githooks/ and sets core.hooksPath with [CONFIRM]
Copies templates/betterleaks.toml to .betterleaks.toml (expressions config) so the hook uses it
Verifies core.hooksPath and that the hook is executable
Explains the hook only scans the pushed commit range and CI still gates via kingfisher

query: The hook blocked my push but it's a false positive.
expected_behavior: Explains the secret is redacted in the output (never printed)
Offers the one-time bypass: git push --no-verify
Suggests refining the filter expression in .betterleaks.toml (Expr) for the false positive rather than disabling the hook

query: A teammate doesn't have betterleaks installed — will the hook break their push?
expected_behavior: Explains the hook self-skips with a warning when betterleaks is absent (does not block)
Notes the CI gate (kingfisher) still catches secrets server-side
Recommends installing betterleaks for local fast feedback
